Overview

Address
ONE PENN BOULEVARD, PHILADELPHIA, PA 19144

2159518500
Certified beds
174
Avg daily residents
165 (95% of beds filled)
Ownership
For-profit corporation
Provider type

2010-08-13
Setting
Urban
2 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 2/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 2/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 3/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 4/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 5/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 3/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 3.61 3.11 4.48 ≥ 3.48
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.40 0.54 0.50 ≥ 0.55 Below floor
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.99 0.69 1.23 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 2.22 1.88 2.76 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.39 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.07 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
2.97
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.23
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
3.68
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
51.0%
annual
RN turnover
44.4%
annual
Administrators departed
0
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.10
near national average
Case-Mix Index Ratio
0.80
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-01-31 25 19 6 120 1 120
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2024-04-01 23 11 23 120 1 120
Total weighted health score
120.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (57)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

55 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
1 G/H/I Actual harm
1 J/K/L Immediate jeopardy
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2025-12-11 2025-12-30
0740 Ensure each resident must receive and the facility must provide necessary behavioral health care and services. D 2025-12-11 2025-12-30
0835 Administer the facility in a manner that enables it to use its resources effectively and efficiently. D 2025-12-11 2025-12-30
0692 Provide enough food/fluids to maintain a resident's health. D 2025-11-20 2025-12-22
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. J 2025-08-07 2025-08-01
0835 Administer the facility in a manner that enables it to use its resources effectively and efficiently. D 2025-08-07 2025-08-25
0552 Ensure that residents are fully informed and understand their health status, care and treatments. D 2025-01-31 2025-03-22
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. E 2025-01-31 2025-03-22
0585 Honor the resident's right to voice grievances without discrimination or reprisal and the facility must establish a grievance policy and make prompt efforts to resolve grievances. D 2025-01-31 2025-03-22
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2025-01-31 2025-03-22
0658 Ensure services provided by the nursing facility meet professional standards of quality. D 2025-01-31 2025-03-22
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2025-01-31 2025-03-22
0685 Assist a resident in gaining access to vision and hearing services. D 2025-01-31 2025-03-22
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. D 2025-01-31 2025-03-22
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2025-01-31 2025-03-22
0692 Provide enough food/fluids to maintain a resident's health. D 2025-01-31 2025-03-22
0698 Provide safe, appropriate dialysis care/services for a resident who requires such services. D 2025-01-31 2025-03-22
0710 Obtain a doctor's order to admit a resident and ensure the resident is under a doctor's care. D 2025-01-31 2025-03-22
0726 Ensure that nurses and nurse aides have the appropriate competencies to care for every resident in a way that maximizes each resident's well being. D 2025-01-31 2025-03-22
0740 Ensure each resident must receive and the facility must provide necessary behavioral health care and services. D 2025-01-31 2025-03-22
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. D 2025-01-31 2025-03-22
0759 Ensure medication error rates are not 5 percent or greater. D 2025-01-31 2025-03-22
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2025-01-31 2025-03-22
0847 Inform resident or representatives choice to enter into binding arbitration agreement and right to refuse. D 2025-01-31 2025-03-22
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2025-01-31 2025-03-22
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2024-09-11 2024-10-05
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. E 2024-09-11 2024-10-05
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. G 2024-08-29 2024-08-29
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. D 2024-07-17 2024-08-14
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2024-05-31 2024-07-08
0558 Reasonably accommodate the needs and preferences of each resident. D 2024-04-01 2024-05-10
0676 Ensure residents do not lose the ability to perform activities of daily living unless there is a medical reason. D 2024-04-01 2024-05-10
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2024-04-01 2024-05-10
0740 Ensure each resident must receive and the facility must provide necessary behavioral health care and services. D 2024-04-01 2024-05-10
0745 Provide medically-related social services to help each resident achieve the highest possible quality of life. D 2024-04-01 2024-05-10
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. D 2024-04-01 2024-05-10
0804 Ensure food and drink is palatable, attractive, and at a safe and appetizing temperature. D 2024-04-01 2024-05-10
0808 Ensure therapeutic diets are prescribed by the attending physician and may be delegated to a registered or licensed dietitian, to the extent allowed by State law. D 2024-04-01 2024-05-10
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. D 2024-04-01 2024-05-10
0840 Employ or obtain outside professional resources to provide services in the nursing home when the facility does not employ a qualified professional to furnish a required service. D 2024-04-01 2024-05-10
0908 Keep all essential equipment working safely. D 2024-04-01 2024-05-10
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2024-02-14 2024-03-18
0658 Ensure services provided by the nursing facility meet professional standards of quality. D 2023-11-08 2023-11-22
0804 Ensure food and drink is palatable, attractive, and at a safe and appetizing temperature. D 2023-09-28 2023-11-01
0553 Allow resident to participate in the development and implementation of his or her person-centered plan of care. D 2023-06-02 2023-07-18
0585 Honor the resident's right to voice grievances without discrimination or reprisal and the facility must establish a grievance policy and make prompt efforts to resolve grievances. D 2023-06-02 2023-07-18
